name: Fortran Optimization Chamber
description: You are standing in a vast chamber that smells of compiled numerical code and hot silicon. The walls are lined with column-major arrays stretching floor to ceiling, their indices glowing faintly in sequential order. A BLAS subroutine hums at the far end, its frequency a perfect A440. The floor is a grid of precomputed constants — SQRT3 and INV_SQRT3 etched into tiles beneath your feet.
look_text: The room is organized by optimization depth. Near the door: introductory tiles on column-major layout and memory access patterns. Center pedestals hold batch processing patterns, their surfaces warm from iterative computation. The far wall displays expert-level coarray parallelism diagrams, half-finished, waiting for someone to complete them. A workbench sits in the corner, its surface scarred from thousands of compilation cycles.

exits.description: A low doorway leads to the bare-metal workshop, where malloc goes to die
Tall bookshelves stretch into the distance, spines labeled with ecosystem names
A spiral staircase ascends to the lattice gallery above
The orange glow of borrow-checker fires flickers through an archway

recipes.name: spectral_batch module
recipes.description: A Fortran module for batch spectral analysis with column-major optimization. Combines batch snap patterns with column-major memory layout for maximum throughput.

recipes.name: coarray_parallel_snap
recipes.description: Parallel snap computation using Fortran coarrays. Projected 3-4× speedup on multi-core hardware. Experimental — no real hardware validation yet.
